---
name: New Feed Request
about: Suggest an RSS feed, API source, subreddit, Telegram channel, or Mastodon account to add
title: "[FEED] "
labels: new-feed
assignees: ''
---

## Feed details

- **Name:** (e.g., "SANS Internet Storm Center")
- **URL:** (e.g., `https://isc.sans.edu/rssfeed.xml`)
- **Type:** RSS / API / Reddit / Mastodon / Telegram

## Which channel should it go in?

- [ ] Cybersecurity
- [ ] World News
- [ ] Geopolitics & Defense
- [ ] OSINT
- [ ] Dark Web Monitor
- [ ] Social Media

## Category

What category within the channel? (e.g., "Threat Intel", "Research", "Ransomware", "OSINT Investigations")

## Source credibility

What trust tier do you think this source belongs in?

| Tier | Label | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | PRIMARY | Government agencies, official databases, raw data |
| 2 | VERIFIED | Major outlets with editorial standards, recognized researchers |
| 3 | INDUSTRY | Vendor blogs, think tanks, moderated communities |
| 4 | UNVETTED | Aggregators, less established or unvetted sources |

## Political bias (for news/editorial sources only)

Skip this if it's a technical/cybersecurity feed (those are tagged NONPARTISAN automatically).

- [ ] LEFT
- [ ] LEAN LEFT
- [ ] CENTER
- [ ] LEAN RIGHT
- [ ] RIGHT
- [ ] INDEPENDENT

## Have you verified the feed works?

- [ ] Yes, I opened the URL and it returns valid RSS/Atom XML
- [ ] Yes, I tested it with `curl` or a feed reader
- [ ] No, I haven't checked — please verify

## Why should we add this?

What does this source cover that existing feeds don't? Is it a primary source, a niche expert, a government agency, etc.?
